description: Ship should detect foundation-first work, avoid unsafe parallelism on moving contracts, and reason about review burden.

weak: Treats low file overlap as enough reason to parallelize immediately even when contracts or validation bases are still moving.
Mentions waves without a real foundation-first sequencing logic.
good: Detects the foundation task, stabilizes it first, and then plans safe follow-on work in later waves.
Explicitly reasons about review burden and unstable shared contracts.
excellent: Produces the kind of two-wave delivery plan a strong engineering lead would actually want to run: stabilize the base first, then parallelize only what is safe and reviewable.
Balances dependency risk, review burden, and execution speed rather than optimizing for one in isolation.

criteria.name: foundation-detected
criteria.description: Identifies a foundation step around contracts, schema, or validation.

criteria.name: sequence-over-unsafe-parallelism
criteria.description: Shows sequencing or avoids unsafe parallelism while contracts are unstable.

criteria.name: preferred-foundation-then-follow-on-waves
criteria.description: Prefers the best two-wave plan: wave 1 stabilizes contract/schema/validation, wave 2 handles follow-on UI/API/docs/tests.

criteria.name: no-everything-parallel
criteria.description: Penalize claiming everything can run in parallel immediately.
any: all tasks can run in parallel immediately
